content.claim: Early tracking in Hungary produces stronger selection effects than in three of four European comparison countries (Austria, Germany, Lithuania, Netherlands), with cross-national evidence showing early tracking increases educational inequality, though effects on mean performance are less robust.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Mean-performance effect is explicitly flagged by authors as 'less clear' than inequality effect; these should not be conflated
Cross-national designs cannot directly measure Hungarian policy-specific causal effects
Comparison is to countries with different institutional histories and contexts
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
